data.body:
PASS-1 REVIEW (T8 — LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe) on the "Repo-snapshot isolation for code-reading gates" plan. The overlay applies: the affected operations are the LLM gates (completion-verifier, plan-review, ticket-review, code-review, spec-scan), each an agentic reviewer prompt with file tools + a typed output schema. Grounded against src/rebar/llm/reviewers/completion_verifier.md and src/rebar/schemas/completion_verdict.schema.json.

AFFIRMATIONS (T8 checks that PASS):
- Enum vocabulary defined: the new `source` enum has its full value vocabulary defined in-plan — exactly two values, attested (default) and local, each with its semantics spelled out ("## What"). No undefined-but-referenced enum.
- Decision rule co-located with the schema it governs: the sign/no-sign rule is bound to the enum where it is defined ("attested ... record verified_at_sha and SIGN over it"; "local ... NEVER sign ... structurally barred from producing a signed verdict"), and AC3 restates it. The rule that needs the enum sits with the enum.
- No ambiguous counter/state increment in the LLM layer.

FINDINGS (2): see the structured JSON. Both are proven instruction-locality / fallback gaps in the agent layer, not generic checklist items:

1. [T8(b)/(e) instruction-locality] The gate prompts today direct the agent to read "the repository" via read-only file tools (completion_verifier.md:23-25 "read-only access to the repository through your file tools"; the verify loop at :59-76 uses list_directory/search_files/read_file with no path root). The plan changes the read TARGET from the working tree to a materialized snapshot dir, but never specifies the locality protocol that points the agent's file tools at that snapshot dir per-run — nor whether the prompt is updated. As written an executor could leave the agent reading the server CWD (the exact footgun) while the operation believes it pinned a SHA. Specify how the snapshot dir is injected as the agent's read root (tool cwd / repo_root binding) and whether the gate prompts need an edit.

2. [T8(d) fallback for failed sub-step] The plan specifies a fallback for ONE failure (private-repo fetch -> descriptive credential error, AC6) but leaves UNspecified the verdict/behavior when attested materialization fails AFTER a successful fetch (read-tree/checkout-index error, extraction error, free-space cap hit mid-gate). Critically it does not state that a failed attested run must NOT silently fall back to `local` (re-introducing the unsigned-vs-signed footgun). Specify the fallback: attested materialization failure -> hard error / no verdict (fail-closed), never an implicit local read.

Note: the `verified_at_sha` binding-into-signed-payload concern is already covered by the G6 and T11 passes (signing-seam + additive-field posture); not re-raised under T8.
